questions.question: What did Rama Natha believe in?
questions.correct_answer: A magic potion that could turn any object into gold
questions.explanation: He thought there was a way to make gold

questions.question: Who helped Rama Natha?
questions.explanation: The sage gave him advice on how to collect the dew

questions.question: What did Madhumati do with the banana crop?
questions.correct_answer: She took it to the market and sold it for a good price
questions.explanation: She helped her husband by selling the bananas

questions.question: How many years did Rama Natha collect dew?
questions.explanation: He spent six years collecting dew from his banana plants

questions.question: What happened when Rama Natha sprinkled the potion on a copper vessel?
questions.correct_answer: Nothing happened, it didn't turn to gold
questions.explanation: The potion didn't work as promised

questions.question: Who called Madhumati to come forward?
questions.explanation: The sage summoned her to reveal the truth

questions.question: What was inside the box that Madhumati brought?
questions.explanation: She revealed the real treasure, not made from the potion

questions.question: Why did Rama Natha go to his large fields after talking to his wife?
questions.correct_answer: To plant banana plants and collect dew
questions.explanation: He started working on the project to collect the dew

questions.question: What was the task that Rama Natha had to complete first?
questions.correct_answer: Planting a banana plant and collecting dew
questions.explanation: He had to take care of the plants and collect the dew

questions.question: How did Madhumati feel about Rama Natha's project?
questions.correct_answer: She was worried because she saw how much money he was spending
questions.explanation: She was concerned about their financial situation

notes.title: Fables and Folk Tales
notes.content: The story is an example of a fable, teaching a moral lesson

notes.title: Magic Potion
notes.content: Rama Natha believed in the magic potion that could turn any object into gold

notes.title: Sage Mahipati
notes.content: The sage helped Rama Natha by giving him advice on how to collect the dew

notes.title: Madhumati's Role
notes.content: Madhumati was involved in helping her husband and revealed the truth about the potion

notes.title: Rama Natha's Mistake
notes.content: Rama Natha wasted six years of his life trying to collect the dew, but it didn't work as promised

notes.title: Banana Plantation
notes.content: Rama Natha created a huge banana plantation with the help of his wife and the sage's advice

notes.title: Dew Collection
notes.content: The dew was collected from the banana plants during winter, but it didn't turn to gold as promised

notes.title: Potion's Failure
notes.content: The magic potion failed to work as promised, turning a copper vessel into gold

notes.title: Revelation of Truth
notes.content: Madhumati revealed the stacks of gold coins that were hidden from Rama Natha

notes.title: Moral Lesson
notes.content: The story teaches a moral lesson about being careful and not getting deceived by false promises

facts.fact: Rama Natha believed in a magic potion that could turn any object into gold
Sage Mahipati helped Rama Natha with his project to collect dew
Madhumati was involved in helping her husband and revealed the truth about the potion
Rama Natha created a huge banana plantation over six years
The dew collection took place during winter months
The magic potion failed to work as promised, turning a copper vessel into gold
Madhumati revealed the stacks of gold coins that were hidden from Rama Natha
Rama Natha wasted six years of his life trying to collect the dew
The story is an example of a fable, teaching a moral lesson
Sage Mahipati called Madhumati to come forward and reveal the truth about the potion
